quiz.question:
White has pushed their pawn to g4. 

We are up a piece, and we want to simplify somehow. 

Playing ...Qf7 comes to mind. 

Is that the correct idea here?

answers.text: After ...Qf7, White  doesn't have to trade the queens and might have some annoying counterplay with their queen instead.

feedback.text: Good job! Actually, after ...Qf7, White has Qa6+, and the only move next is to give up our queen with ...Qf6!

distractors.text: That's a good idea. We are up a piece. The queen is also nicely placed on the f-file, and could potentially create some mating attacks later.

feedback.text: ...Qf7 is a huge blunder here! White has Qa6+ and the only move is to play ...Qf6, giving up the queen.
